description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dark reddish-brown fuselage and dual stacked wings, each adorned with black cross insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine area features visible exhaust pipes and structural details. The tail section is painted in a muted greenish-blue and includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, also marked with cross insignias. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The overall design is compact, with a robust, hard-surfaced exterior and minimal decorative elements.
description: A vintage, dark reddish-brown biplane with dual wings, black cross insignias, an open cockpit, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the propeller, landing gear, and the cross insignias on the upper wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the greenish-blue tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and horizontal stabilizers with insignias.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the tail skid, open cockpit, and the structure of the lower and upper wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat.
